info.title: Native Update — Public Management API
info.summary: Run an app's whole lifecycle with a personal access token — apps, API keys, signing keys, keystore backups, builds, and releases.
info.description:
Everything the Native Update dashboard does — create and configure apps, mint and rotate API keys, manage a signing key, back up a keystore, upload a bundle, publish it, promote it between channels, adjust a rollout — over HTTP. Intended for CI, scripts, and agents.

**Authentication:** `Authorization: Bearer nu_pat_…`. An access token is a user-level secret with no origin restrictions: keep it in a CI secret or server environment variable, never in a browser or a repository. This plane sends no CORS headers on purpose.

**Not the same as an app API key.** `nu_app_…` keys authenticate the plugin inside your app against `/api/v1/updates/check`; they are rejected here.

**Scoping:** a token reaches the apps selected for it in the dashboard, or — when created with `all_apps` — every app you own, including apps created later. Creating an app with an explicitly-scoped token auto-attaches it. Anything out of scope answers 404 — never 403 — so the API cannot be used to discover other people's apps.

**Permissions:** every valid token may `manage` (read; create and edit apps; mint, rotate and restrict API keys; create and rotate a signing key; upload, publish, promote and roll out builds; upload and download a keystore). The three delete permissions — `builds.delete`, `apps.delete`, `keys.delete` — are opt-in and off by default.

**Access tokens are dashboard-only.** There is no endpoint to create, rotate, or delete a token here; a leaked token can never mint another token or widen its own reach.

**Uploads are queued:** POSTing a bundle returns 202 with a job id. Poll `GET /jobs/{jobId}` until `completed` or `failed`. A 202 means accepted, not live.

tags.name: Apps
tags.description: Create, read, edit, and delete apps the token may manage

tags.name: API keys
tags.description: Mint, rotate, reveal, restrict, and retire app API keys

tags.name: Signing key
tags.description: The app's bundle-signing key (public key + fingerprint only)

tags.name: Builds
tags.description: Upload, publish, promote, and retire builds

get.description: What this token is and which apps it manages. Call this first: it saves a client from guessing app ids and reports whether deletion is permitted. Never returns the secret.

post.description: A token created for specific apps auto-attaches the new app to itself, so it can manage the app immediately.

bundle_id.description: Reverse-DNS package id, globally unique across the platform. Immutable once set.

409.description: That bundle_id already belongs to another app

error.message: The bundle id 'com.example.app' is already registered to another app.

422.description: The plan's app quota is full

error.message: App limit reached for this plan.

patch.description: Edits name, platform, and description. `bundle_id` is immutable and is not accepted here.

delete.description: Requires a token with the `apps.delete` permission. Permanent and cascading — removes the app's builds and API keys.

get.description: Newest first. An unknown channel or status is refused with 422 rather than silently ignored.

post.description:
Always queued: signing and storing a bundle is too slow to hold a request open. Returns 202 with a job id — poll `GET /jobs/{jobId}` until `completed`. The build exists immediately as `processing` and is invisible to devices until the job finishes.

Rate limited to 30 uploads per hour.

Duplicate and non-monotonic versions are refused synchronously, before any job exists.

file.description: The bundle .zip. Max 100 MB by default.

version.description: Strict semver. Must be newer than the channel's active head.

mandatory.description: Force the update on eligible devices.

202.description: Accepted for processing — not yet live

409.description: That version already exists in the channel

error.message: Version 1.2.0 already exists in production channel.

422.description: Invalid payload, or the version is not newer than the channel head

error.message: Version 1.1.0 is not newer than the current production head (1.2.0). Bump the version or use the explicit rollback flow.

500.description: The bundle could not be accepted for processing

patch.summary: Pause, resume, archive, or edit a build
patch.description: Only `active` builds are served to devices, so setting `paused` stops a bad release on the next update check. Setting `status` to `active` on a build still `uploading`, `processing`, or `failed` is refused with 422 `BUILD_NOT_READY` — a build only becomes active when its upload job completes.

422.description: Invalid payload, or the build is not ready to be made active

error.message: This build is still processing and cannot be made active yet.

delete.description: Requires a token with the `builds.delete` permission. Permanent. The stored bundle is cleaned up in the background; a build promoted elsewhere keeps working, because promotions share one stored object.

post.summary: Promote a build into another channel
post.description: Zero-copy: the new build points at the same stored bundle, and `promoted_from_id` points back at the source.

201.description: The new build in the target channel

409.description: That version already exists in the target channel

422.description: The build is already in the target channel

error.message: Build is already in the target channel.

patch.summary: Set a build's rollout percentage, or pause/resume it
patch.description: Ramp a release: 10 → 25 → 50 → 100. Send `rollout_percentage`, `enabled`, or both. `0` halts the rollout; `enabled: false` pauses it without changing the percentage.

get.summary: List an app's API keys (safe fields only)

200.description: The app's keys — never the secret

post.description: Returns the full `plaintext_key` once (also retrievable later via reveal). An app allows at most 3 active keys.

error.message: This app already has the maximum of 3 active API keys. Rotate or retire one first.

429.description: Rate limited — the plane cap, or a per-key action cap

error.message: Too many key actions. Try again shortly.

get.summary: Get one API key (safe fields only)

delete.description: Requires a token with the `keys.delete` permission.

post.description: Issues a new secret and invalidates the old one immediately.

error.message: This key predates recovery support — rotate it to get a new copyable key.

patch.summary: Set or clear a key's client restrictions
patch.description: Locks a key to web origins / Android apps / iOS apps. An empty body clears every restriction (the key becomes unrestricted). Origins are scheme- and port-exact; http:// only for local hosts.

post.summary: Mark an API key for retirement (it keeps working)

get.summary: Get the app's signing key (public key + fingerprint)
get.description: Absent = `{configured:false}`, a 200 — not a 404. The private key is never returned.

409.description: The app already has an active signing key

error.message: This app already has an active signing key. Rotate it instead of creating another.

409.description: No active signing key to rotate

error.message: No active signing key to rotate. Create one first.

get.description: Absent = `{configured:false}`, a 200. Never returns the file contents.

post.summary: Upload or replace the keystore backup
post.description: The file must be a .zip, at most 10 MB. Stored privately; uploading again replaces the previous backup.

error.message: The keystore backup must be a .zip file (zip your keystore before uploading — an added layer of protection).

get.description: Streams the stored .zip from private storage — no public URL is ever exposed.

404.description: No keystore backup for this app (or the app is out of scope)

error.message: No keystore backup for this app.

get.description: Poll every ~5 seconds until `completed` or `failed`. A job is visible to any token that manages its app, so rotating a token never strands an in-flight deploy.

accessToken.description: A personal access token created in the dashboard (Access Tokens). Server/CI only — it has no origin restrictions. `X-Access-Token: nu_pat_…` is accepted as a fallback when your transport owns the Authorization header.

App.name: app
App.description: Numeric id or the string app_id. Prefer app_id in CI — it survives re-creating an app.

ApiKeyResponse.description: The API key, safe fields only

PlaintextKey.description: The API key including its full secret — shown here and on reveal only

SigningKeyResponse.description: The signing key, public fields only

KeyNotFound.description: API key absent, or the app is outside this token's scope

error.message: API key not found for this app.

Unauthorized.description: Missing, malformed, unknown, revoked, or expired token

error.message: An access token is required. Send it as "Authorization: Bearer nu_pat_…" — create one in the dashboard (Access Tokens).

wrongFamily.summary: An app API key was sent instead of an access token

error.message: Invalid access token format. Tokens look like nu_pat_… — create one in the dashboard (Access Tokens). App API keys (nu_app_…) authenticate the device update plane (/api/v1) and are not accepted here.
Access token is expired or revoked. Rotate it in the dashboard (Access Tokens).

PermissionDenied.description: The token lacks an opt-in permission

error.message: This access token does not have the 'builds.delete' permission. Grant it in the dashboard (Access Tokens), or use a token that has it.

NotFound.description: Absent, **or** outside this token's apps — deliberately indistinguishable, so the API cannot be used to discover other people's apps. If an app id you trust 404s, check `GET /token` first.

error.message: App not found, or this access token does not manage it. Check the app id, and the token's app list in the dashboard (Access Tokens).

RateLimited.description: 120 requests/minute, or 30 uploads/hour. Retry after the `Retry-After` header.

BuildStatus.description: Devices are only ever served `active` builds.

permissions.description: The effective set. `manage` is implied by holding a valid token; the three delete permissions are opt-in.

all_apps.description: When true, the token reaches every app you own — including apps created later — and `apps` is just the current snapshot.

promoted_from_id.description: Set when this build was promoted from another channel

attempts.description: A failing job retries 3 times with backoff.

error.description: On failure: why it gave up.

ApiKeySafe.description: An app API key with safe fields only — never the secret.

recoverable.description: Whether reveal can return the plaintext.

KeyRestrictions.description: Client restrictions for an API key. An empty object clears all restrictions.

origins.description: Allowed web origins. Scheme- and port-exact; http:// only for local hosts. Exact host or https://*.example.com wildcard.

SigningKey.description: Public fields only — the private key never leaves the server. When absent, only `configured: false` is returned.

KeystoreMeta.description: Metadata of the private keystore .zip backup — never the file itself. When absent, only `configured: false` is returned.
